title: Nalpathaneeswaram Sree Mahadeva Temple
tagline: Nalpathaneeswaram Sree Mahadeva Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Nalppathenneeswaram Sree Mahadeva Temple is a Mahadevar temple situated in Nalppathenneswaram, in Panavally village of Cherthala taluk in Alleppy district of Kerala state; this pla
overview.description: Nalppathenneeswaram Sree Mahadeva Temple is a Mahadevar temple situated in Nalppathenneswaram, in Panavally village of Cherthala taluk in Alleppy district of Kerala state; this place is 20 kilometers from Cherthala and 11 kilometers from Aroor on the Cherthala Arookutty bus route. Here Lord Siva is in Kiratha Bhava. It is considered the 48th foundlings of Saint Khara, one who founds the Ettumanur temple and Kaduthuruthi temple. The temple is artistically constructed in a quiet, village atmosphere.

topPlaces.name: Thrichattukulam Mahadeva Temple
topPlaces.description: Thrichattukulam Mahadeva Temple (Malayalam: തൃച്ചാറ്റുകുളം മഹാദേവക്ഷേത്രം) is an ancient Hindu temple dedicated to Lord Shiva is situated on the banks of the Vembanad Lake at Panavally Island of Alleppey District in Kerala state in India. The Thrichattukulam Mahadeva Temple is one of the three important shiva Temple in Karappuram Kingdom (Malayalam: കരപ്പുറം രാജ്യം) in Kerala, those are Velorvattom Sri Mahadeva Temple, Nalpathaneeswaram Sree Mahadeva Temple and Thrichattukulam Mahadeva Temple.

topPlaces.name: Kakkathuruth
topPlaces.description: Kakkathuruth is a tiny island located at the Alappuzha district in the Indian state of Kerala surrounded by the Vembanatu Lake. Known as the Island of crows, the National Geographic described the sunset seen from Kakkathuruthu as the best in the world.

topPlaces.name: Panavally
topPlaces.description: Panavally(പാണാവള്ളി) is a village in India that is located in the northern part of Cherthala taluk and Alappuzha district in the state of Kerala. It is about a 25-minute (19 km) drive from Kochi city.

topPlaces.name: Vembanad
topPlaces.description: Vembanad (Malayalam: [ʋeːmbɐnaːɖə̆]) is the longest lake in India, and the largest in the state of Kerala. The lake has an area of 2,033 square kilometres (785 sq mi) and a maximum length of 96.5 km (60 mi).

topPlaces.name: Perumbalam Bridge
topPlaces.description: The Perumbalam bridge, also known as Perumbalam-Panavally Bridge is a bow string arch bridge in Alappuzha district in Kerala, India. It was opened to the public on 07 March 2026.

topPlaces.name: Ezhupunna
topPlaces.description: Ezhupunna is a village in the taluk of Cherthala in Alappuzha district in the Indian state of Kerala. It lies between Alappuzha and Kochi, near National Highway 66.

topPlaces.name: Chandiroor
topPlaces.description: Chandiroor is a village in Alappuzha district, Kerala, India. It is situated on National Highway 66 (India) between Eramalloor and Aroor.

topPlaces.name: Kuthiathode
topPlaces.description: Kuthiathode is a gram panchayat in Alappuzha district in the Indian state of Kerala. It is only 20 minutes from Kochi city.

items.note: Thrichattukulam Mahadeva Temple (Malayalam: തൃച്ചാറ്റുകുളം മഹാദേവക്ഷേത്രം) is an ancient Hindu temple dedicated to Lord Shiva is situated on the banks of the Vembanad Lake at Panavally Island of Alleppey District in Kerala state in India. The Thrichattukulam Mahadeva Temple is one of the three important shiva Temple in Karappuram Kingdom (Malayalam: കരപ്പുറം രാജ്യം) in Kerala, those are Velorvattom Sri Mahadeva Temple, Nalpathaneeswaram Sree Mahadeva Temple and Thrichattukulam Mahadeva Temple.

Kakkathuruth is a tiny island located at the Alappuzha district in the Indian state of Kerala surrounded by the Vembanatu Lake. Known as the Island of crows, the National Geographic described the sunset seen from Kakkathuruthu as the best in the world.

Panavally(പാണാവള്ളി) is a village in India that is located in the northern part of Cherthala taluk and Alappuzha district in the state of Kerala. It is about a 25-minute (19 km) drive from Kochi city.

Vembanad (Malayalam: [ʋeːmbɐnaːɖə̆]) is the longest lake in India, and the largest in the state of Kerala. The lake has an area of 2,033 square kilometres (785 sq mi) and a maximum length of 96.5 km (60 mi).
The Perumbalam bridge, also known as Perumbalam-Panavally Bridge is a bow string arch bridge in Alappuzha district in Kerala, India. It was opened to the public on 07 March 2026.
Ezhupunna is a village in the taluk of Cherthala in Alappuzha district in the Indian state of Kerala. It lies between Alappuzha and Kochi, near National Highway 66.
Chandiroor is a village in Alappuzha district, Kerala, India. It is situated on National Highway 66 (India) between Eramalloor and Aroor.
Kuthiathode is a gram panchayat in Alappuzha district in the Indian state of Kerala. It is only 20 minutes from Kochi city.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Nalpathaneeswaram Sree Mahadeva Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Nalpathaneeswaram Sree Mahadeva Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Nalpathaneeswaram Sree Mahadeva Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (36 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (16 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Nalpathaneeswaram Sree Mahadeva Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Nalpathaneeswaram Sree Mahadeva Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Nalpathaneeswaram Sree Mahadeva Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Nalpathaneeswaram Sree Mahadeva Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Nalpathaneeswaram Sree Mahadeva Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Thrichattukulam Mahadeva Temple, Kakkathuruth, Panavally, Vembanad, Perumbalam Bridge.
